paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs
summary: Conjecture 9.1 from arXiv:2506.07264 proposes that for a unicyclic graph of order n whose unique cycle has odd length k, the sign of the inequalities between s⁺(G), n, and s⁻(G) is determined by k mod 4. No follow-up paper resolving or partially proving this conjecture was found in a web search conducted in May 2026. The closely related arXiv:2604.15656 (April 2026) concerns p-energies for p=3 (cube of eigenvalues) rather than p=2, and does not address this conjecture.
search_queries: unicyclic graph "square energy" "positive square energy" s+ s- odd cycle conjecture 2025
Akbari Mohar Pragada Zhang "positive square energy" unicyclic graphs 2025 arxiv
notes: No follow-up found. The conjecture is very recent (June 2025). The paper arXiv:2604.15656 (April 2026) by overlapping authors studies positive and negative p-energies for p=3, not p=2, so it is unlikely to directly address Conjecture 9.1. Absence of follow-up is expected for a conjecture of this age.
